faq.question: Why is my appliance stuck "Waiting for features to finish initialising"?
faq.answer:
This log message indicates that the plugin has identified the appliance in your Home Connect account but is unable to communicate with it to retrieve its current status, settings, or capabilities. This typically occurs because the appliance is offline or has lost its connection to the Home Connect cloud servers.

To resolve this issue, perform the following diagnostic steps:
1. Open the official Home Connect app on your mobile device.
2. Disable Wi-Fi on your phone to force a cellular/mobile data connection. If you cannot control the appliance in the official app over mobile data, the appliance is not correctly connected to the Home Connect servers.
3. Check the appliance's network settings within the Home Connect app; it should ideally show three green lines indicating a strong connection.
4. If the appliance appears offline, try power-cycling the device (unplugging it or turning off the circuit breaker for 30 seconds) or re-pairing it with your Wi-Fi network.

The plugin will automatically finish initialising the features once it can successfully establish a connection to the appliance via the Home Connect API.
faq.semantic_abstract: Appliance initialisation timeouts occur when the Home Connect API can identify an appliance associated with an account but cannot establish a real-time connection to fetch device states or features. This typically results from the physical appliance being offline or disconnected from the manufacturer's cloud servers, preventing the plugin from completing the HomeKit service mapping.
